name: verification-process
description: Sigstore bundle verification process per attestation.md

verification_steps.name: step2_verify_bundle
verification_steps.description: Verify Sigstore bundle against trusted root using sigstore-go
components: Certificate chain validation against Fulcio CA
Transparency log entry verification against Rekor

verification_steps.name: step3_extract_statement
verification_steps.description: Decode and parse the in-toto statement from dsseEnvelope.payload

verification_steps.name: step5_verify_subject
verification_steps.description: Verify subject[0].name is 'manifest.json' and digest matches

verification_steps.name: step6_verify_pack_digest
verification_steps.description: Verify predicate.pack_digest matches computed pack digest

verification_steps.name: step7_verify_identity
verification_steps.description: Optionally verify certificate identity matches expected signer
verification_steps.note: Verifiers SHOULD configure expected signer identities (email or OIDC URI)

signing_process.name: authenticate_via_oidc
signing_process.description: Authenticate with OIDC provider (e.g., GitHub, Google)

signing_process.name: obtain_certificate
signing_process.description: Obtain short-lived signing certificate from Fulcio

signing_process.name: construct_statement
signing_process.description: Create in-toto statement with manifest digest as subject

signing_process.name: sign_statement
signing_process.description: Sign statement using ephemeral private key

signing_process.name: record_to_rekor
signing_process.description: Record signature to Rekor transparency log

signing_process.name: create_bundle
signing_process.description: Create Sigstore bundle with certificate chain and tlog entry

notes: Verification uses sigstore-go against the public Sigstore trusted root (Fulcio CA, Rekor public key). Certificate identity verification is policy-driven and configured by the verifier.
